name: Sierra de Ojos-Albos Trail
name_original: Cueva Mingubela desde Ojos-Albos — circular por el río Voltoya
description: Hike the 9.5-mile Cueva Mingubela from Ojos-Albos circular by the Río Voltoya, a moderate route with ancient rock art and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Ojos-Albos offers trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and open plains. The region features varied terrain, including areas near the río Voltoya and the Embalse de Serones. Hikers can explore paths that lead to viewpoints and historical sites like dolmens, providing diverse experiences. Best hiking trails around Ojos-Albos The most popular hiking route is La Atalaya y Cueva Mingubela — circular desde Ojos-Albos, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views from La Atalaya. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Embalse de Serones from Ojos-Albos — circular by the río Voltoya, a difficult 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. This trail explores the area around the Embalse de Serones and the Voltoya river. Local hikers also love the Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through open terrain to a historical dolmen, often completed in about 46 minutes. Hiking around Ojos-Albos is defined by rolling hills, river paths, and open landscapes featuring historical dolmens. The network offers options for easy hikes as well as more challenging routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Ojos-Albos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Ojos-Albos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ojos-Albos?
A: Ojos-Albos offers a diverse selection of &lt;strong&gt;9 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt;. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the region's varied landscape.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect to see while hiking in Ojos-Albos?
A: The hiking trails in Ojos-Albos traverse a landscape characterized by &lt;strong&gt;rolling hills, serene river valleys, and expansive open plains&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter areas near the río Voltoya and the Embalse de Serones, along with historical sites like ancient dolmens, offering a rich and varied experience.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Ojos-Albos has &lt;strong&gt;3 easy hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326180807/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is only 1.8 miles (2.9 km) long and leads through open terrain to a historical dolmen.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ojos-Albos?
A: Yes, several routes in Ojos-Albos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1136626621/la-atalaya-y-cueva-mingubela-circular-desde-ojos-albos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Atalaya y Cueva Mingubela — circular desde Ojos-Albos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) trail offering views from La Atalaya.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The region features historical dolmens, and you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4846071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Azálvaro Bike Path&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are all nearby.

Q: Is Ojos-Albos suitable for family hikes?
A: Yes, Ojos-Albos offers trails that are suitable for families. The easier routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326180807/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de las Cruces Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them enjoyable for children and adults alike. Always check the specific trail details for length and difficulty to match your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: The trails in Ojos-Albos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from river paths to open landscapes, and the opportunity to discover historical sites like dolmens.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Ojos-Albos?
A: The best time to hike in Ojos-Albos is typically during &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt; when the weather is mild and comfortable. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can also be suitable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Embalse de Serones or the Voltoya river?
A: Absolutely. One notable route that explores this area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326114753/the-embalse-de-serones-from-ojos-albos-circular-by-the-rio-voltoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Embalse de Serones from Ojos-Albos — circular by the río Voltoya&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging 13.0-mile (20.9 km) path offers extensive views and experiences around the reservoir and along the river.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trail, Ojos-Albos is a rural area, and parking is generally available near trailheads or in the villages. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for potential parking information or look for designated spots in Ojos-Albos village.

Q: Are there any moderate trails for hikers looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, Ojos-Albos features &lt;strong&gt;5 moderate trails&lt;/strong&gt; that provide a good balance of challenge and accessibility. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326126993/sierra-de-ojos-albos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ojos-Albos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, covering 9.5 miles (15.3 km) with notable elevation changes, offering a more engaging experience.
tips: A small town with less than 80 inhabitants, the municipality is characterized by the nearby Sierra de Ojos Albos, to the south. It is part of the Central Site and beyond, there is the plain that goes in a northerly direction.

Mingubela Cave, also known as Peña Mingubela, is a fascinating rock shelter located near the small village of Ojos Albos in Ávila, Spain. This site is known for its ancient cave paintings, which depict various figures, including warriors, some of whom are shown holding shields or in prayer positions. The cave is divided by a stone column, which adds to its unique structure.

If you are interested in visiting, it is important to note that the cave is only accessible on foot and the path can be quite steep, so it is advisable to bring a flashlight and seek precise directions from locals. Additionally, there are several hiking trails in the area, such as the circular trail from Ojos Albos that includes La Atalaya and Mingubela Cave.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty level. It's suitable for hikers with good fitness. While some paths are easily accessible, sure-footedness is required on certain sections due to varied terrain and significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Cueva Mingubela from Ojos-Albos trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, typical of moderate mountain hiking. The route navigates the area around the Peña Mingubela, an imposing outcrop, and follows the Río Voltoya. Hikers should be prepared for paths that require careful footing.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most remarkable feature is the Cueva Mingubela Rock Art, a natural shelter with significant post-Paleolithic rock paintings. You'll also pass through the picturesque Ojos-Albos Village Center and enjoy views of the Río Voltoya and its confluence with the Arroyo Valdelaguila.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva Mingubela from Ojos-Albos — circular by the Río Voltoya trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally open to dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Be mindful of the varied terrain and ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter might present colder temperatures and potentially icy sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located in the Sierra de Ojos-Albos, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the general trails. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially if planning to access specific protected areas or historical sites.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Ojos-Albos. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for trails originating from villages like Ojos-Albos Village Center to have public parking options nearby. We recommend checking local maps or Komoot's app for precise trailhead and parking locations.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of Cueva Mingubela?
faqs.answer: The Cueva Mingubela Rock Art is highly significant, featuring post-Paleolithic rock paintings depicting human figures, animals, and geometric patterns. These artworks were created over thousands of years, from the Bronze Age to the Second Iron Age and even the Medieval Era, suggesting it served as a crucial cultic site or sanctuary for rituals.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and appropriate layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as is a first-aid kit. Don't forget a camera to capture the scenic views and rock art!

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Ojos-Albos?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts from the Ojos-Albos Village Center, you may find local amenities such as small cafes or traditional pubs. These can be great spots to refuel before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check their operating hours in advance.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and requirement for sure-footedness, this trail might be challenging for very young children or families new to hiking. It's best suited for families with older children who have some hiking experience and good physical fitness.
